title: Python runtime source blocker audit

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by auditing the remaining source-owned runtime blockers and selecting the next safe generated-runtime transition slice.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not delete or relocate runtime source until generated outputs, command_package_ir bindings, package imports, tests, and package data prove the replacement boundary; do not claim full completion from an audit.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Which blocker group is the next smallest implementation slice and what proof is required before source removal.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A blocker requires broad product-runtime relocation across workspace, memory, and planning in a single slice or would leave installed console scripts broken.
canonical_core.next_action: Classify direct imports of source runtime blockers, update #892 decomposition with the transition order, and promote the next executable relocation slice.
completion_criteria: Remaining source runtime blockers are classified by import owner and transition risk.
The #892 decomposition records the next safe implementation order.
A bounded follow-on implementation slice is promoted, or the audit records why promotion is blocked.
No full #892 completion claim is made from the audit alone.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Advance #892 by auditing the remaining source-owned runtime blockers and selecting the next safe generated-runtime transition slice.
intent.constraints: Do not delete or relocate runtime source until generated outputs, command_package_ir bindings, package imports, tests, and package data prove the replacement boundary; do not claim full completion from an audit.
intent.latitude: Which blocker group is the next smallest implementation slice and what proof is required before source removal.
intent.escalation: A blocker requires broad product-runtime relocation across workspace, memory, and planning in a single slice or would leave installed console scripts broken.

execution.next_step: Classify direct imports of source runtime blockers, update #892 decomposition with the transition order, and promote the next executable relocation slice.
execution.proof: Planning summary remains clean and generated package static proof still passes.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

required_continuation.activation trigger: Promote the next generated-runtime relocation slice.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Identified the remaining direct source-runtime import blockers and selected workspace generated-runtime relocation as the next safe implementation slice.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Planning summary and generated package static proof remain clean.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: No validation remains for this audit slice; implementation proof belongs to the promoted relocation slice.

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Classify the remaining source runtime blocker imports and record the next generated-runtime relocation order.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning execplan, python-generated-cli decomposition, planning state, and mutation provenance.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Fill in an expected upper bound before implementation starts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace summary --target . --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Audit remaining #892 source runtime blockers and promote the next generated-runtime relocation slice.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract
post_decomposition_delegation.route skipped reason: This slice is an architectural dependency audit before deletion or relocation; it needs repo-wide import evidence and cannot be safely delegated as mechanical implementation yet.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium if audit is mistaken for implementation

delegation_outcome_feedback.route skipped reason: This slice is an architectural dependency audit before deletion or relocation; it needs repo-wide import evidence and cannot be safely delegated as mechanical implementation yet.
delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: Do not delete shipped source files until generated outputs and package entrypoints prove they are unused or replacement-owned.
delegation_outcome_feedback.decomposition adjustment: Promote source-runtime blocker audit before attempting full #892 completion promotion.

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Planning summary clean and generated package static proof passed before the audit slice was closed.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The audit identifies the remaining blocker groups and transition order; it does not remove the blockers.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: remaining source runtime blockers classified and next transition order recorded
execution_summary.resume from: create or continue workspace generated-runtime relocation execplan

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The audit only classifies blockers and records order; full #892 remains open.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Direct imports show workspace generated outputs still depend on agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives, planning outputs still depend on repo_planning_bootstrap installer/runtime_projection, and memory outputs still depend on repo_memory_bootstrap installer/runtime_primitives.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if the selected transition order is invalidated by generated package proof or package import evidence.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

discard: Raw import listing details after classification.
continuation: Promote workspace generated-runtime relocation as the next #892 implementation slice.
